title: Local Input Security, Resource Limits, Privacy, and Diagnostics · Runtime Lens: Privacy Diagnostics
summary: Imported mazes allocate from untrusted dimensions, pickle is accepted, paths escape their root, and logs retain player data indefinitely. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the privacy diagnostics increment in bounded local-data threat model and controls. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.
Keep imported data and package resources within explicit authorities and avoid user-controlled executable lookup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 5068 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-security-local-files-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-security-local-files-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_security_local_files_2(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1:
        return False, admitted_cells, "resource-shape"
    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:
        return False, rejected_documents, "rejection-evidence"
    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"

# Evidence: maze-privacy-diagnostics-199p7u
# Competency: maze-privacy-diagnostics.
# Case 8722: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_privacy_diagnostics_199p7u(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_199p7u = "199p7u-u7p991-8722"
    assert evidence_variant_199p7u
    evidence_axes_199p7u = (
        "grid199p7u",
        "topology8722",
        "searchu7p991",
        "event199p7u8722",
        "access8722199p7u",
        "recoveryu7p9918722",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_199p7u)) == 6
    if admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1:
        return False, admitted_cells, "resource-shape"
    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:
        return False, rejected_documents, "rejection-evidence"
    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Privacy Diagnostics model · Theory Privacy Diagnostics, keep Privacy Diagnostics connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Privacy Diagnostics model · Theory Privacy Diagnostics, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Privacy Diagnostics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Privacy Diagnostics model · Theory Privacy Diagnostics, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1830 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; the prior good state must survive failure; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Privacy Diagnostics model · Theory Privacy Diagnostics, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Verbose logs are harmless because they stay on the same computer.
